definitions.gloss_en: a stat representing the offensive power or damage output of a unit
definitions.gloss_jp: {ユニット|ゆにっと}の{攻|こう}{勢|せい}{的|てき}な{強|つよ}さや{与|あた}えられるダメージ{量|りょう}を{示|しめ}す{ステータス|すてーたす}
definitions.explanation_en: A numeric stat on a unit's profile measuring how much damage or effectiveness the unit delivers in combat. The primary offensive stat.
definitions.implicit_notes: 力 (ryoku) as a stat suffix signals a capacity or power level. 攻撃力 is the standard JRPG and SRPG term for the ATK stat.
definitions.orthography_notes: 攻撃力: 攻撃 (attack) + 力 (power/capacity). Universal Japanese game term. Fire Emblem uses 攻撃力 (ATK) as a core stat. Weblio dictionary lists it as a general game/military term. Not WH-specific.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Universal across all Japanese game genres. Fire Emblem uses 攻撃力 as the ATK stat. In miniature wargames, may appear as a damage value or wound roll modifier.

usage_jp: {攻|こう}{撃|げき}{力|りょく}ステータス (ATK stat); {攻|こう}{撃|げき}{力|りょく}が{高|たか}いユニット (high attack-power unit)
comparative_notes: 防御 (bougyo, defense) is the antonym stat. 攻撃数 (kougeki-suu) counts attacks; 攻撃力 measures how effective each attack is.

examples.japanese: {攻|こう}{撃|げき}{力|りょく}が{防|ぼう}{御|ぎょ}{値|ち}を{超|こ}えた{場|ば}{合|あい}、ダメージが{発|はっ}{生|せい}する。
examples.english: Damage occurs when attack power exceeds the defense value.

cross_references.note: 攻撃数 — number of attacks (quantity, not power)
攻撃 — the action; 攻撃力 is the stat
